texto: She's been trained to put that moment off as long as she could stand and fight, to slash and burn, and take out as many as humanly possible. Because under the strength, the wit and the endless training was the knowledge that some day, some way, she wouldn't be fast enough, smart enough, lucky enough.And the monster would win.
explicacao: Citação de Dance of the Gods sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “She's been trained to put that moment off as long as she could stand and fight, to slash and burn…” articula sabedoria, blair, fighting em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como dia-chuvoso e termino.

frase_original: She's been trained to put that moment off as long as she could stand and fight, to slash and burn, and take out as many as humanly possible. Because under the strength, the wit and the endless training was the knowledge that some day, some way, she wouldn't be fast enough, smart enough, lucky enough.And the monster would win.

texto: Men are vile creatures, really. using women up, casting them aside. Leaving them along and broken, Better to leave them first, isn't it? Better yet to pay them back, and leave them bleeding,Sick and tired of being the one left behind, aren't you? And all the fighting, all the death. I can help you with that. I'd so like to help you.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Dance of the Gods: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Men are vile creatures, really. using women up, casting them aside. Leaving them along and broken…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, blair, lilith pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e lilith.

Better yet to pay them back, and leave them bleeding,Sick and tired of being the one left behind, aren't you? And all the fighting, all the death. I can help you with that. I'd so like to help you.

texto: Cian smiled a little, but it didn't reach those cool blue eyes. 'Whether it's by violence or nature, the result is the same. I've seen more death than you, more than you ever will. But still, you've seen more than most humans have or will. And that separates us, you and me, from the rest.''We don't have any choice about that.''Oh, of course we do. I know a bit about loneliness, and what can chase it back, even for the short run.
explicacao: Citação de Dance of the Gods sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Cian smiled a little, but it didn't reach those cool blue eyes. 'Whether it's by violence or natu…” articula amizade, blair, cian em registro de amizade, com relevância para contextos como luto e cian.
